step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the conclusion provided by @Agent2. Haversian fibers are indeed correctly identified as being outside the scope of living pulp. These fibers form an essential component of the mineralized dentin, providing strength and structure to the tooth, whereas the living pulp contains collagenous connective tissue, blood vessels, nerves, and cells such as odontoblasts and fibroblasts – none of these include the rigid Haversian system seen deeper in the dentin layers.

Therefore, my clear answer based on the given information is still **B**: Haversian fibres.
raw_response:
I agree with the conclusion provided by @Agent2. Haversian fibers are indeed correctly identified as being outside the scope of living pulp. These fibers form an essential component of the mineralized dentin, providing strength and structure to the tooth, whereas the living pulp contains collagenous connective tissue, blood vessels, nerves, and cells such as odontoblasts and fibroblasts – none of these include the rigid Haversian system seen deeper in the dentin layers.

Therefore, my clear answer based on the given information is still **B**: Haversian fibres.
